sections.title: 1. N+1 Query Patterns
2. Unnecessary React Re-renders
3. Caching Opportunities
4. Memory Leaks
5. Redundant Computations
Summary Table
sections.content:
| Issue | File | Severity | Impact |
|---|---|---|---|
| `collectionGroup` scan in `getUserGlobalStats` | `gameDataService.ts:124` | **Critical** | O(all users) per profile load |
| `readScoreRows` called per game, not shared | `api/leaderboard/route.ts:69` | **High** | Up to 21× full scans |
| Jose Rizal: read-then-write on every score | `PlayGameClient.tsx:119` | **Medium** | 2 Firestore ops → 1 |
| Game sort runs inline (no `useMemo`) | `profile/page.tsx:337` | **Medium** | Wasted CPU every render |
| Announcements filtered twice | `library/page.tsx:309` | **Low** | Minor CPU waste |
| `maxValue` computed 50× inside map | `leaderboard/page.tsx:380` | **Low** | Minor CPU waste |
| 6 cascading effects on library mount | `library/page.tsx` | **Medium** | 6+ re-renders per mount |
| 60s leaderboard TTL | `api/leaderboard/route.ts:26` | **Medium** | Excess Firestore reads |
| Game visibility not cached | `library/page.tsx:74` | **Medium** | Firestore read per page load |
| Unbounded `profileCache` | `leaderboard/page.tsx:16` | **Low** | Slow memory growth |
| Auth async unmount guard missing | `AuthContext.tsx:86` | **Low** | Stale state updates |

The single biggest win is fixing `getUserGlobalStats` to use the already-cached leaderboard API data instead of scanning all Firestore score documents on every profile view.

codeBlocks.code:
// Current (bad): full scan on every profile load
const allScoresSnap = await getDocs(collectionGroup(db, 'scores'))
const CACHE_TTL_SECONDS = 300  // 5 minutes instead of 60
const profileCache: Record<string, UserProfile> = {}  // grows forever
const entries: GlobalLeaderboardEntry[] = Object.entries(userTotalMatches).map(...)
